description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone block with a rough, weathered surface and a distinct, curved groove pattern running along one side. The block is predominantly brown with subtle variations in tone, and its edges are uneven and chipped, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. One face is relatively flat, while the opposite side features pronounced textural ridges and indentations, possibly from tooling or natural erosion. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and a granular texture is evident across its surfaces. There are some small, white alphanumeric markings on one flat side, likely for cataloging or identification purposes. The overall form and finish suggest it could have served as a barrier or boundary element in an urban or landscaped setting.
description: A weathered, brown stone block with a rough, grooved surface and irregular, chipped edges. One side is flat with visible catalog markings, while the other features pronounced ridges and indentations.
description_view_0: This view highlights the curved groove pattern and rough, textured surface on the most detailed side of the block.
description_view_1: This side view shows the block's thin profile and reveals the flatness of one face, with visible edge chipping.
description_view_2: The flat face is prominent here, displaying the white catalog markings and the block's overall irregular outline.
description_view_3: This angle emphasizes the depth of the grooves and the block's uneven, jagged edges.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or garden landscaping
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows significant roughness, chipped edges, and some erosion, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: White alphanumeric catalog markings are visible on one flat face; no decorative engravings present.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely to be placed adjacent to or stacked with other similar blocks as part of a boundary or edging.
